authors: Thomson SJ, Tavakkolizadeh M, Love-Jones S, Patel NK, Gu JW, Bains A, Doan Q, Moffitt M
title: Effects of Rate on Analgesia in Kilohertz Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation: Results of the PROCO Randomized Controlled Trial
study_question: How does 1-10 kHz SCS frequency affect analgesia and charge usage?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic low back pain, with or without leg pain, undergoing SCS.
outcome_measures: ED-NRS pain, charge per second, ODI, EQ-5D-5L, PSQI, PGIC
inclusion_criteria: Chronic low back pain, prior pain care, stable opioids, ODI 20-80, morphine under 180 mg/day
answer_to_study_question: 1-10 kHz provided equivalent pain relief; 1 kHz used significantly less charge.
